pka_cmd_desc_t Struct Reference
[MiCA PKA Device API (gxio/pka.h)]

A PKA command descriptor. More...


Data Fields

pka_vector_t vec_a
 Vector A.
pka_vector_t vec_b

pka_vector_t vec_e
 Vector E.
uint32_t tag
 Tag to copy unmodified to the result descriptor.
uint32_t hw_len_a: 9
 Indicates the length in 4-byte words of some or all of the operands.
uint32_t rsvd_1: 7
 Reserved.
uint32_t hw_len_b: 9
 Indicates the length in 4-byte words of operands not described by len_a.
uint32_t rsvd_2: 5
 Reserved.
uint32_t command: 8
 The command to perform.
uint32_t rsvd_3: 8
 Reserved.
uint32_t enc_vectors_bitmask: 6
 Indicates which of the five input parameter vectors contains encrypted data that must be decrypted before use.
uint32_t kdr: 2
 Which of the (up to) four Key Decryption Keys must be used to decrypt the input parameter vectors specified by the enc_vectors_bitmask.
uint32_t odd_powers: 5
 For shift left/right basic operations, the number of bits to shift (in the range 0 - 31).
uint32_t driver_status: 2
 Indicates the state of a descriptor block (empty, command or result).
uint32_t linked: 1
 Indicates whether the next command may not be executed before this one has finished execution.

Detailed Description

A PKA command descriptor.

Contains operands, a tag, a command that indicates the operation to perform with the operands, and other fields pertinent to the operation of the commmand.

A command may require up to five operands. If a command does not require a particular operand the fields of that vector should be set to 0. The actual commands are described in the I/O Device Guide (UG404).

Field Documentation

pka_vector_t pka_cmd_desc_t::vec_a

Vector A.

May contain multiple operands, depending on the command.

pka_vector_t pka_cmd_desc_t::vec_b

Vector B.

May contain multiple operands, depending on the command.

pka_vector_t pka_cmd_desc_t::vec_c

Vector C.

May contain multiple operands, depending on the command.

pka_vector_t pka_cmd_desc_t::vec_d

Vector D.

May contain multiple operands, depending on the command.

pka_vector_t pka_cmd_desc_t::vec_e

Vector E.

May contain multiple operands, depending on the command.

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::tag

Tag to copy unmodified to the result descriptor.

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::hw_len_a

Indicates the length in 4-byte words of some or all of the operands.

Some operand lengths may be described by len_b.

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::rsvd_1

Reserved.

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::hw_len_b

Indicates the length in 4-byte words of operands not described by len_a.

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::rsvd_2

Reserved.

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::command

The command to perform.

The list of commands and the description of the operations they perform is in I/O Device Guide (UG404).

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::rsvd_3

Reserved.

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::enc_vectors_bitmask

Indicates which of the five input parameter vectors contains encrypted data that must be decrypted before use.

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::kdr

Which of the (up to) four Key Decryption Keys must be used to decrypt the input parameter vectors specified by the enc_vectors_bitmask.

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::odd_powers

For shift left/right basic operations, the number of bits to shift (in the range 0 - 31).

For modular exponentiation (with and without CRT) operations, the number of odd powers to use (in the range 1 - 16).

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::driver_status

Indicates the state of a descriptor block (empty, command or result).

uint32_t pka_cmd_desc_t::linked

Indicates whether the next command may not be executed before this one has finished execution.
